entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: A legume crop (Cicer arietinum L.) studied for nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: NITROGEN FIXATION
entities.description: The process of converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia by the plant.

entities.name: N ACCUMULATION
entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen accumulated in the plant tissues.

entities.name: NODULATION
entities.description: The formation of root nodules by symbiotic bacteria.

entities.name: ACETYLENE REDUCTION ACTIVITY
entities.description: A biochemical measure used as a proxy for nitrogenase activity and nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: ISOTOPE-DILUTION METHOD
entities.description: A technique using 15N isotopes to estimate the amount of nitrogen fixed from the air.

entities.name: SAFFLOWER
entities.description: A non-nitrogen-fixing plant used as a reference control in the isotope-dilution method.

entities.name: N UPTAKE
entities.description: The absorption of nitrogen from the soil by the plant.

entities.name: 15N FERTILIZER
entities.description: A labeled nitrogen fertilizer used in isotope dilution studies.

entities.name: K 850
entities.description: A specific genotype of chickpea evaluated for nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: G 130
entities.description: A specific genotype of chickpea evaluated for nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: NODULE MASS
entities.description: The total weight of the root nodules.

entities.name: RHIZOBIUM SP.
entities.description: A genus of bacteria that forms symbiotic relationships with legumes to fix nitrogen.

entities.name: INOCULATION
entities.description: The process of introducing Rhizobium bacteria into the plant root system.
